title: Quiz: Vectors and Vector Spaces
description: Test your understanding of vectors and vector spaces in quantum computing

questions.question: What is the dimension of a vector space spanned by the standard basis vectors |0⟩ and |1⟩?
questions.explanation: The vector space spanned by |0⟩ and |1⟩ is 2-dimensional. These two vectors form a basis, and any qubit state can be written as α|0⟩ + β|1⟩.

questions.question: Which of the following is a valid column vector representation of |1⟩?
questions.explanation: |1⟩ is represented as [0, 1]ᵀ (column vector with 0 in first position, 1 in second). |0⟩ is [1, 0]ᵀ.

questions.question: For vectors u = [1, 2, 3]ᵀ and v = [4, 5, 6]ᵀ, what is u + v?
The zero vector is an element of every vector space.
questions.explanation: True! The zero vector is required by the vector space axioms. It serves as the additive identity: v + 0 = v for any vector v.

questions.question: In quantum computing, the state space of n qubits has dimension n.
questions.explanation: False! The state space of n qubits has dimension 2ⁿ, not n. For example, 2 qubits have a 4-dimensional state space with basis |00⟩, |01⟩, |10⟩, |11⟩.

questions.question: Which property is NOT required for a set V with operations + and · to be a vector space?
questions.explanation: Scalar multiplication doesn't need to be commutative. The axioms require: closure, associativity, identity elements, inverses, and distributivity. Vector spaces don't define multiplication between two vectors as a basic operation.

questions.question: Which of the following are examples of vector spaces? (Select all that apply)

options.text: The set of positive real numbers

questions.explanation: a) ✓ ℂ² is the standard 2D complex vector space. b) ✓ Matrices form a vector space under matrix addition and scalar multiplication. c) ✗ Positive reals aren't closed under scalar multiplication (negative scalars). d) ✓ Polynomials form a vector space (can add and scale them).
